| Cytoplasmic | -0.019 |
| Transporter | 0.510 |
| Rhodopsin | 0.429 |

Interpretation:

- Cytoplasmic proteins show **no scaling with species abundance**.
- Transporters and rhodopsins **increase with species abundance**.

---

## Rest group

| Protein class | slope |
|---------------|------|
| Cytoplasmic | 0.204 |
| Transporter | 0.431 |
| Rhodopsin | 0.275 |

Interpretation:

- All classes increase with species abundance.
- Transporters show the strongest scaling.

---

# Pairwise slope comparisons

Slope differences were tested using **Tukey-adjusted contrasts**.

### Why this test is used

When multiple slopes are estimated from the same model, pairwise comparisons require adjustment to control the family-wise error rate.

Tukey correction is appropriate for comparing all pairs.

---

## Oligo-like group

| Comparison | Result |
|-----------|--------|
| Cytoplasmic vs Transporter | highly significant difference |
| Cytoplasmic vs Rhodopsin | significant difference |
| Transporter vs Rhodopsin | not significant |

Interpretation:

- Transporters and rhodopsins scale similarly.
- Both scale much more strongly than cytoplasmic proteins.

---

## Rest group

| Comparison | Result |
|-----------|--------|
| Cytoplasmic vs Transporter | significant difference |
| Cytoplasmic vs Rhodopsin | not significant |
| Transporter vs Rhodopsin | not significant |

Interpretation:

- Transporters scale more strongly than cytoplasmic proteins.
- Rhodopsin scaling is intermediate.

---

# Model assumptions

The relationship between:
log10(Protein abundance) and log10(Species abundance)


should be approximately linear.

Because abundance relationships often follow power laws, this assumption is typically reasonable.

---

### 2. Residual normality

Residuals should be approximately normally distributed.

Evaluated using:

- Q–Q plots
- residual histograms

---

### 3. Homoscedasticity

Residual variance should not systematically increase with fitted values.

Evaluated using:

- residual vs fitted plots
- residual vs logA plots

---

### 4. Independence

Observations are assumed independent **conditional on the random effects**.

This is addressed by including species and date random intercepts.

---

# Why the model is appropriate

Several factors support the validity of this approach:

- Large dataset (**29k observations**)
- Multiple species and sampling dates
- Log transformation stabilizes variance
- Mixed-effects structure accounts for repeated observations
- Scaling relationships are biologically meaningful
- Slopes are estimated and compared using established statistical methods

Therefore, fitting a **log–log linear mixed model** provides a robust framework for testing whether protein classes differ in how their abundances scale with species abundance.

```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(lme4)
  library(lmerTest)
  library(emmeans)
  library(readr)
  library(patchwork)
})

# ============================================================
# PARAMETERS
# ============================================================
rhodo_kos <- c("K04641", "K04643")

PSEUDO_A <- 1e-12
PSEUDO_P <- 1e-6

pdf_file_main <- "Protein_Expression_EmpiricalSpread_Overview_Transporter_Cytoplasmic_Rhodopsin.pdf"
stats_file    <- "Protein_Expression_EmpiricalSpread_Transporter_Cytoplasmic_Rhodopsin_stats.txt"
diag_pdf_file <- "Protein_Expression_EmpiricalSpread_Model_Diagnostics.pdf"

# empirical ribbon settings
n_bins          <- 24   # x-axis bins per Group2 x ProteinClass
min_points_bin  <- 5    # minimum observations needed per bin

# ============================================================
# HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ============================================================

num_stats <- function(x) {
  tibble(
    min    = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = max(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean   = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr    = IQR(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

safe_p_line <- function(pairs_tbl, group_name, labelA, labelB) {
  x1 <- pairs_tbl %>%
    filter(Group2 == group_name, contrast == paste(labelA, "-", labelB))

  x2 <- pairs_tbl %>%
    filter(Group2 == group_name, contrast == paste(labelB, "-", labelA))

  if (nrow(x1) == 1) {
    return(sprintf("%s vs %s: p = %s", labelA, labelB, signif(x1$p.value[1], 3)))
  }
  if (nrow(x2) == 1) {
    return(sprintf("%s vs %s: p = %s", labelA, labelB, signif(x2$p.value[1], 3)))
  }
  sprintf("%s vs %s: p = NA", labelA, labelB)
}

safe_slope_line <- function(slopes_tbl, group_name, class_name) {
  x <- slopes_tbl %>%
    filter(Group2 == group_name, ProteinClass == class_name)

  if (nrow(x) == 1) {
    sprintf("%s slope = %.3f", class_name, x$logA.trend[1])
  } else {
    sprintf("%s slope = NA", class_name)
  }
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixed-effect prediction grid
# line = fitted mean from the mixed model
# no uncertainty ribbon here; spread is shown empirically
# ------------------------------------------------------------
make_prediction_grid <- function(model, dat, n = 200) {

  grid <- dat %>%
    group_by(Group2, ProteinClass) %>%
    summarise(
      logA_min = quantile(logA, 0.02, na.rm = TRUE),
      logA_max = quantile(logA, 0.98, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    rowwise() %>%
    do({
      tibble(
        Group2 = .$Group2,
        ProteinClass = .$ProteinClass,
        logA = seq(.$logA_min, .$logA_max, length.out = n)
      )
    }) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(
      Group2 = factor(Group2, levels = levels(dat$Group2)),
      ProteinClass = factor(ProteinClass, levels = levels(dat$ProteinClass))
    )

  X <- model.matrix(~ logA * ProteinClass * Group2, data = grid)
  beta <- fixef(model)

  grid$fit <- as.numeric(X %*% beta)
  grid
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Empirical spread ribbons
# These summarize observed data spread within x-bins
#
# outer ribbon: 10th to 90th percentile
# inner ribbon: 25th to 75th percentile
# median line : empirical median (optional)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
make_empirical_spread <- function(dat, n_bins = 24, min_points_bin = 5) {

  dat %>%
    group_by(Group2, ProteinClass) %>%
    mutate(
      x_bin = cut(logA, breaks = n_bins, include.lowest = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    group_by(Group2, ProteinClass, x_bin) %>%
    summarise(
      x_mid = mean(logA, na.rm = TRUE),
      x_min = min(logA, na.rm = TRUE),
      x_max = max(logA, na.rm = TRUE),
      y_q10 = quantile(logP, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),
      y_q25 = quantile(logP, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
      y_med = quantile(logP, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),
      y_q75 = quantile(logP, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
      y_q90 = quantile(logP, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_bin = n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    filter(n_bin >= min_points_bin) %>%
    arrange(Group2, ProteinClass, x_mid)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Diagnostic plots on transformed scale
# ------------------------------------------------------------
write_model_diagnostics <- function(model, dat, out_pdf) {
  res <- resid(model)
  fit <- fitted(model)

  pdf(out_pdf, width = 8, height = 8)
  par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(4.5, 4.5, 2, 1))

  plot(
    fit, res,
    pch = 16, cex = 0.6,
    xlab = "Fitted values",
    ylab = "Residuals",
    main = "Residuals vs fitted"
  )
  abline(h = 0, lty = 2)

  qqnorm(res, pch = 16, cex = 0.6, main = "Normal Q-Q")
  qqline(res, lty = 2)

  hist(
    res, breaks = 40,
    main = "Residual histogram",
    xlab = "Residuals"
  )

  plot(
    dat$logA, res,
    pch = 16, cex = 0.6,
    xlab = "log10(Species abundance)",
    ylab = "Residuals",
    main = "Residuals vs logA"
  )
  abline(h = 0, lty = 2)

  dev.off()
}

# ============================================================
# 1) SPECIES GROUPS
# ============================================================
species_groups2 <- species_meta_rhodopsin %>%
  select(Species_ID, Group_For_Plots) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  mutate(Group2 = ifelse(Group_For_Plots == "Oligo_like", "Oligo-like", "Rest")) %>%
  select(Species_ID, Group2)

# ============================================================
# 2) PROTEIN ANNOTATION MAP
# ============================================================
prot_annot <- gene_table_rhodopsin %>%
  select(Protein_ID, KEGG_ID, KEGG_Subcategory, PHOBIUS_Annotation) %>%
  distinct()

# ============================================================
# 3) MAIN ANALYSIS TABLE
#
# CLASS PRIORITY:
#   1. Rhodopsin
#   2. Transporter
#   3. Cytoplasmic
# ============================================================
dat <- protein_table_rhodopsin %>%
  left_join(prot_annot, by = c("Protein_ID", "KEGG_ID")) %>%
  left_join(
    species_abundance_rhodopsin %>%
      select(Species_ID, Date, Species_Abundance),
    by = c("Species_ID", "Date")
  ) %>%
  left_join(species_groups2, by = "Species_ID") %>%
  filter(
    Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm > 0,
    Species_Abundance > 0,
    !is.na(KEGG_ID),
    KEGG_ID != "",
    KEGG_ID != "Unclassified",
    !is.na(Group2)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ProteinClass = case_when(
      KEGG_ID %in% rhodo_kos ~ "Rhodopsin",
      grepl("Transporter", KEGG_Subcategory, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Transporter",
      PHOBIUS_Annotation == "CytoplasmaticProtein" ~ "Cytoplasmic",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    ProteinClass = factor(
      ProteinClass,
      levels = c("Cytoplasmic", "Transporter", "Rhodopsin")
    ),
    Group2 = factor(Group2, levels = c("Oligo-like", "Rest")),
    logA = log10(Species_Abundance + PSEUDO_A),
    logP = log10(Protein_Abundance_IBAQ_Norm + PSEUDO_P)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(ProteinClass))

# ============================================================
# 4) QC
# ============================================================
qc_counts <- dat %>%
  count(Group2, ProteinClass)

qc_summary <- tibble(
  metric = c("N_obs", "N_species", "N_proteins", "N_dates"),
  value = c(
    nrow(dat),
    n_distinct(dat$Species_ID),
    n_distinct(dat$Protein_ID),
    n_distinct(dat$Date)
  )
)

cat("\n===== QC =====\n")
print(qc_counts)
print(qc_summary)

# ============================================================
# 5) MIXED MODEL
# ============================================================
m <- lmer(
  logP ~ logA * ProteinClass * Group2 + (1 | Species_ID) + (1 | Date),
  data = dat,
  REML = FALSE
)

model_summary <- summary(m)
model_anova   <- anova(m)

cat("\n===== MIXED MODEL SUMMARY =====\n")
print(model_summary)

cat("\n===== MIXED MODEL ANOVA =====\n")
print(model_anova)

# ============================================================
# 6) SLOPES PER CLASS WITHIN GROUP
# ============================================================
slopes <- emtrends(m, ~ ProteinClass | Group2, var = "logA")
slopes_tbl <- as.data.frame(slopes)

pairs_tbl <- as.data.frame(
  pairs(slopes, by = "Group2", adjust = "tukey")
)

cat("\n===== SLOPES (d logP / d logA) =====\n")
print(slopes_tbl)

cat("\n===== SLOPE COMPARISONS (within Group2; Tukey) =====\n")
print(pairs_tbl)

# ============================================================
# 7) PANEL ANNOTATION TEXT
# ============================================================
ann_df <- tibble(
  Group2 = levels(dat$Group2),
  ann = c(
    paste(
      safe_slope_line(slopes_tbl, "Oligo-like", "Cytoplasmic"),
      safe_slope_line(slopes_tbl, "Oligo-like", "Transporter"),
      safe_slope_line(slopes_tbl, "Oligo-like", "Rhodopsin"),
      safe_p_line(pairs_tbl, "Oligo-like", "Transporter", "Rhodopsin"),
      safe_p_line(pairs_tbl, "Oligo-like", "Cytoplasmic", "Rhodopsin"),
      safe_p_line(pairs_tbl, "Oligo-like", "Cytoplasmic", "Transporter"),
      sep = "\n"
    ),
    paste(
      safe_slope_line(slopes_tbl, "Rest", "Cytoplasmic"),
      safe_slope_line(slopes_tbl, "Rest", "Transporter"),
      safe_slope_line(slopes_tbl, "Rest", "Rhodopsin"),
      safe_p_line(pairs_tbl, "Rest", "Transporter", "Rhodopsin"),
      safe_p_line(pairs_tbl, "Rest", "Cytoplasmic", "Rhodopsin"),
      safe_p_line(pairs_tbl, "Rest", "Cytoplasmic", "Transporter"),
      sep = "\n"
    )
  )
)

# ============================================================
# 8) FITTED LINES + EMPIRICAL SPREAD RIBBONS
# ============================================================
grid <- make_prediction_grid(m, dat, n = 200)

spread_dat <- make_empirical_spread(
  dat = dat,
  n_bins = n_bins,
  min_points_bin = min_points_bin
)

# ribbon QC summary
spread_bin_summary <- spread_dat %>%
  group_by(Group2, ProteinClass) %>%
  summarise(
    n_bins_retained = n(),
    min_bin_n       = min(n_bin, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_bin_n       = max(n_bin, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_bin_n      = mean(n_bin, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_bin_n    = median(n_bin, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ============================================================
# 9) GLOBAL AXIS LIMITS
# ============================================================
x_limits <- c(-1.2, 1.6)
y_limits <- c(2, 8)

x_breaks <- seq(-1, 1.5, by = 0.5)
y_breaks <- seq(2, 8, by = 2)

x_labels <- sprintf("%.1f", x_breaks)
x_labels[x_labels == "-0.0"] <- "0.0"

ann_x <- -1.18
ann_y <- 7.95

# ============================================================
# 10) BASE PLOT FUNCTION
#
# ribbons now show observed spread of the data:
# outer ribbon = 10th-90th percentile
# inner ribbon = 25th-75th percentile
# solid line   = fitted mixed-model mean
# dashed line  = empirical median within bins
# ============================================================
make_group_plot <- function(group_name, show_x_axis = TRUE) {

  ann_sub <- ann_df %>%
    filter(Group2 == group_name)

  ggplot() +
    # outer empirical spread
    geom_ribbon(
      data = spread_dat %>% filter(Group2 == group_name),
      aes(x = x_mid, ymin = y_q10, ymax = y_q90, fill = ProteinClass),
      alpha = 0.10
    ) +

    # inner empirical spread
    geom_ribbon(
      data = spread_dat %>% filter(Group2 == group_name),
      aes(x = x_mid, ymin = y_q25, ymax = y_q75, fill = ProteinClass),
      alpha = 0.22
    ) +

    # empirical median
    geom_line(
      data = spread_dat %>% filter(Group2 == group_name),
      aes(x = x_mid, y = y_med, color = ProteinClass),
      linewidth = 0.7,
      linetype = "22"
    ) +

    # fitted mixed-model line
    geom_line(
      data = grid %>% filter(Group2 == group_name),
      aes(x = logA, y = fit, color = ProteinClass),
      linewidth = 1.1
    ) +

    # annotation
    geom_text(
      data = ann_sub,
      aes(label = ann),
      x = ann_x,
      y = ann_y,
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      hjust = 0,
      vjust = 1,
      size = 3.1
    ) +

    scale_color_manual(
      values = c(
        "Cytoplasmic" = "#1f77b4",
        "Transporter" = "#ff7f0e",
        "Rhodopsin"   = "#d62728"
      ),
      drop = FALSE
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c(
        "Cytoplasmic" = "#1f77b4",
        "Transporter" = "#ff7f0e",
        "Rhodopsin"   = "#d62728"
      ),
      drop = FALSE
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = x_breaks,
      labels = x_labels
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      breaks = y_breaks,
      labels = y_breaks
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(
      xlim = x_limits,
      ylim = y_limits,
      expand = FALSE
    ) +
    labs(
      title = group_name,
      x = if (show_x_axis) "log10(Species abundance)" else NULL,
      y = "log10(Protein abundance iBAQ)",
      color = "Protein class",
      fill  = "Protein class"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "top",
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey85", linewidth = 0.5),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.line = element_line(color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.4),
      axis.title.x = if (show_x_axis) element_text() else element_blank(),
      axis.text.x  = if (show_x_axis) element_text() else element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x = if (show_x_axis) element_line(color = "grey50") else element_blank()
    )
}

# ============================================================
# 11) BUILD THE TWO ALIGNED PLOTS
# ============================================================
p_oligo <- make_group_plot("Oligo-like", show_x_axis = FALSE)
p_rest  <- make_group_plot("Rest", show_x_axis = TRUE)

# ============================================================
# 12) STACK THEM WITH A SHARED LEGEND
# ============================================================
p_combined <- p_oligo / p_rest +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect", heights = c(1, 1)) &
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.box = "horizontal"
  )

print(p_combined)

ggsave(
  pdf_file_main,
  p_combined,
  width = 8,
  height = 9,
  device = pdf,
  bg = "white"
)

# ============================================================
# 13) MODEL DIAGNOSTICS
# ============================================================
write_model_diagnostics(m, dat, diag_pdf_file)

# ============================================================
# 14) WRITE STATS FILE
# ============================================================
sink(stats_file)

cat("============================================================\n")
cat("Protein expression mixed model statistics\n")
cat("Rhodopsin vs Transporter vs Cytoplasmic\n")
cat("Empirical spread ribbons + fitted mixed-model lines\n")
cat("============================================================\n\n")

cat("Files written by this script:\n")
cat(" -", pdf_file_main, "\n")
cat(" -", diag_pdf_file, "\n")
cat(" -", stats_file, "\n\n")

cat("============================================================\n")
cat("CLASSIFICATION RULES\n")
cat("============================================================\n\n")
cat("Priority order used for classification:\n")
cat("1. Rhodopsin   : KEGG_ID in rhodo_kos\n")
cat("2. Transporter : KEGG_Subcategory contains 'Transporter'\n")
cat("3. Cytoplasmic : PHOBIUS_Annotation == 'CytoplasmaticProtein'\n")
cat("Proteins are assigned once only, using this priority order.\n\n")

cat("============================================================\n")
cat("PLOT INTERPRETATION\n")
cat("============================================================\n\n")
cat("Solid line  : fitted mixed-model relationship on log10-log10 scale\n")
cat("Dashed line : empirical median log10(Protein abundance) within x-bins\n")
cat("Inner ribbon: empirical 25th-75th percentile of observed log10(Protein abundance)\n")
cat("Outer ribbon: empirical 10th-90th percentile of observed log10(Protein abundance)\n")
cat("Ribbons show observed data spread and are NOT confidence intervals of the fitted line.\n\n")

cat("Empirical ribbon construction parameters:\n")
cat(" - number of x-bins per Group2 x ProteinClass =", n_bins, "\n")
cat(" - minimum observations required per retained bin =", min_points_bin, "\n\n")

cat("============================================================\n")
cat("QC COUNTS\n")
cat("============================================================\n\n")
print(qc_counts)
cat("\n")
print(qc_summary)
cat("\n\n")

cat("============================================================\n")
cat("RIBBON BIN SUMMARY\n")
cat("============================================================\n\n")
print(spread_bin_summary)
cat("\n\n")

cat("============================================================\n")
cat("MIXED MODEL SUMMARY\n")
cat("============================================================\n\n")
print(model_summary)
cat("\n\n")

cat("============================================================\n")
cat("MIXED MODEL ANOVA\n")
cat("============================================================\n\n")
print(model_anova)
cat("\n\n")

cat("============================================================\n")
cat("SLOPES (EMTRENDS)\n")
cat("============================================================\n\n")
print(slopes_tbl)
cat("\n\n")

cat("============================================================\n")
cat("PAIRWISE SLOPE CONTRASTS WITHIN GROUP (TUKEY)\n")
cat("============================================================\n\n")
print(pairs_tbl)
cat("\n\n")

cat("============================================================\n")
cat("ANNOTATION TEXT USED IN FIGURE\n")
cat("============================================================\n\n")
print(ann_df)
cat("\n\n")

cat("============================================================\n")
cat("MODEL INTERPRETATION NOTES\n")
cat("============================================================\n\n")
cat(
  paste(
    "The mixed model was fitted on log10-transformed abundance variables.",
    "This implies a linear relationship on the transformed scale and a power-law relationship on the original scale.",
    "Slope differences were tested using emtrends(), which estimates class-specific trends of logP versus logA within each Group2 level.",
    "These slopes are scaling exponents on the log-log scale.",
    "The plotted ribbons do not come from model uncertainty; they summarize the empirical distribution of observed data within local x-bins.",
    sep = "\n"
  )
)
cat("\n\n")

cat("============================================================\n")
cat("MODEL ASSUMPTION CHECKLIST\n")
cat("============================================================\n\n")
cat(
  paste(
    "Inspect the diagnostics PDF and verify:",
    "1. Residuals vs fitted: no strong curvature or funnel pattern.",
    "2. Q-Q plot: no severe deviation from normality in residuals.",
    "3. Residuals vs logA: no remaining systematic trend.",
    "4. No single class/group combination with extreme leverage due to very small n.",
    "",
    "If these checks look acceptable, the log-log linear mixed model and slope contrasts are defensible.",
    "The empirical ribbons are descriptive visual summaries of observed spread and do not rely on model-distribution assumptions.",
    "",
    "If assumptions are poor, possible next steps include:",
    "- class-specific variance structures (e.g. nlme::lme with varIdent),",
    "- robust mixed modelling,",
    "- or restricting inference to the abundance range shared by all classes.",
    sep = "\n"
  )
)
cat("\n\n")

sink()

cat("\nDone.\n")
cat("Main plot    :", pdf_file_main, "\n")
cat("Diagnostics  :", diag_pdf_file, "\n")
cat("Stats        :", stats_file, "\n")
```

# Output fasta files with rhodopsins for alignment
```{r}
############################################################
# Export expressed rhodopsins as FASTA
# Header format:
# Species_ID_Group2_Phylum_Class_Order_Family_Genus_Species
#
# Assumes:
# - protein_table_rhodopsin contains expressed proteins
# - gene_table contains KEGG_ID and protein sequence
# - species_meta contains taxonomy
# - protein sequence column is named Protein_Sequence
#   (change below if your column has a different name)
############################################################

library(dplyr)

# Rhodopsin KO IDs
rhodo_kos <- c("K04643", "K04641")

############################################################
# 1) Identify expressed rhodopsins
############################################################

rhodo_expressed <- protein_table_rhodopsin %>%
  filter(KEGG_ID %in% rhodo_kos) %>%
  distinct(Species_ID, Protein_ID)

############################################################
# 2) Add sequence, taxonomy, and Oligo-like vs Rest category
############################################################

rhodo_fasta_df <- rhodo_expressed %>%
  left_join(
    gene_table %>% select(Protein_ID, Protein_Sequence),
    by = "Protein_ID"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    species_meta %>%
      select(Species_ID, Group_For_Plots, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species),
    by = "Species_ID"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Group2 = ifelse(Group_For_Plots == "Oligo_like", "Oligo-like", "Rest"),
    fasta_header = paste(
      Species_ID,
      Group2,
      Phylum,
      Class,
      Order,
      Family,
      Genus,
      Species,
      sep = "_"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Protein_Sequence))

############################################################
# 3) Write FASTA
############################################################

fasta_file <- "Expressed_Rhodopsins_Group2.fasta"
con <- file(fasta_file, "w")

for (i in seq_len(nrow(rhodo_fasta_df))) {
  writeLines(paste0(">", rhodo_fasta_df$fasta_header[i]), con)
  writeLines(rhodo_fasta_df$Protein_Sequence[i], con)
}

close(con)

cat("FASTA exported:", fasta_file, "\n")
```
